topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on the screen, a jarring intrusion in the quiet afternoon. A name flashed: 'Rico_The_Legend'. A cold knot tightened in my stomach as I hovered over the 'Accept' button. My hands began to tremble slightly, the cheap plastic of my phone slick under my grip. I stared at the profile picture—a smirk that felt instantly familiar, sharp and unwelcome. Every instinct screamed at me to block, to delete the entire account, but a strange, heavy inertia kept my finger poised above the confirmation. I took a deep, shaky breath, the air catching painfully in my chest. What did I even expect? The silence in my room felt thick, suffocating, as if the air itself had turned to lead.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone with the deliberate slowness of someone handling something fragile. The sight of the request sent a wave of nausea through me. It was him. Mark. The memory of his sneering face from the schoolyard flooded my mind, bringing a hot flush to my cheeks. I let the phone rest on my desk, the sound of the notification echoing loudly in the otherwise empty apartment. I traced the outline of the screen with my thumb, feeling a profound sense of dread settle deep in my bones. Why now? There was nothing to say, no reason to engage, yet the urge to retreat, to disappear entirely, was overwhelming. I just wanted the noise to stop.

[story 3]

The chime was sharp, slicing through the dull drone of my evening. I glanced at the screen, and a sudden, sharp ache bloomed behind my eyes. It was him. That idiot. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. I didn't move immediately, just sat there, frozen, my gaze locked onto the profile picture. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips, a sound that held more exhaustion than actual breath. My shoulders slumped forward, a physical manifestation of the weight pressing down on my chest. Accepting felt like signing a contract I desperately wanted to void, a surrender to a history I was trying to outrun.
